steps.title: Use the center in standard form
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ has equation $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$. With center $(-3,2)$, this becomes
$$(x+3)^2+(y-2)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Find the radius value
steps.content:
Use the point $(-9,10)$ on the circle to find the distance squared from the center.
$$r^2=(-9-(-3))^2+(10-2)^2$$
$$r^2=(-6)^2+8^2$$
$$r^2=36+64=100$$

steps.title: Write the circle equation
steps.content: Substitute $r^2=100$ into the standard form. $$(x+3)^2+(y-2)^2=100$$ The answer is B.
